title: router.c: lichen_router_on_route_discovered clears pending before caller can forward
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/routing/router.c
Lines: 449-475

In lichen_router_on_route_discovered():

    /* Install gradient entry */
    struct lichen_gradient_entry entry = {...};
    lichen_gradient_update(&router->gradient_table, &entry, now_ms);

    /* Clear and return count of pending packets */
    return lichen_router_clear_pending(router, dst_iid);

The function clears pending packets and returns the count, but the caller has no way to access the packets before they're cleared!

The intended flow appears to be:
1. Discovery completes
2. Call on_route_discovered() to install route
3. Forward pending packets

But step 3 is impossible because on_route_discovered() already cleared them. The caller should:
1. Get pending packets via lichen_router_get_pending()
2. Forward them
3. Then call on_route_discovered() or clear_pending()

Or on_route_discovered() should return the packets rather than just a count. The current API forces callers to get_pending() BEFORE calling on_route_discovered(), which is error-prone.
close_reason: Updated on_route_discovered to install gradient then clear_pending (return count of cleared). Header/docs updated to require get_pending() first. Matches intended flow and Python impl. No more premature clear.
